git_write: Git operations that modify the working tree or index
git_remote_write: Remote git mutations (gh pr merge, gh issue create, git push)
package_run: Run package scripts (npm run, npx, just)
lang_exec: Execute code via language runtimes or shell-sourced scripts (python, node, source)
container_read: Read-only container and image inspection (logs, inspect, stats, ps)
container_write: Container state mutations (start, stop, build, tag, create)
container_exec: Execute or copy data in containers (exec, run, attach, cp)
container_destructive: Destructive container operations (docker rm, docker system prune)
service_read: Read-only service inspection (systemctl status, cat, journalctl)
service_write: Service and systemd mutations (restart, enable, daemon-reload)
browser_read: Read-only browser inspection (snapshots, screenshots, console, network, assertions)
browser_interact: In-page browser interactions (click, type, resize, mouse, navigation controls)
browser_state: Browser state mutations (cookies, storage, routes, console/network state)
browser_navigate: Navigate a browser page to a new URL
browser_exec: Execute arbitrary code in the browser page context
browser_file: Browser actions that read from or write to the host filesystem
db_write: Write operations on databases (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, DROP, ALTER)
agent_read: Read-only agent CLI metadata, status, help, or generated output
agent_write: Agent CLI state mutations without launching a coding run
agent_exec_read: Launch a local agent run intended for inspection or review
agent_exec_write: Launch a local agent run that can edit workspace state
agent_exec_remote: Submit or continue an agentic run in a remote agent service
agent_server: Start an agent protocol server or app server
agent_exec_bypass: Launch an agent run while explicitly bypassing approvals or sandboxing
unknown: Unrecognized command or tool — not in any classify table
